rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary: English Conversation I (Eikaiwa I) is a required course for first-year students designed to help you use English in real-life situations. In this class, the teacher acts as a "coach" rather than a lecturer, focusing on helping you practice speaking as a physical and social skill.
aims: Many students in Japan feel nervous when they speak English. They worry about making mistakes, so they often stay quiet. If you do not practice talking, your English fluency will not improve. This course is designed to help you stop being quiet and start communicating. You will learn that you already have enough English skills to talk about real-life topics. The goal of English Conversation I is to improve your daily speaking and listening skills. We focus on real communication and "spoken grammar" rather than just studying rules from a textbook. Moreover,
goals: Practice talking to others and understanding what they say in everyday situations.
Improve your pronunciation so that people can so that people understand you easily.
Practice using the grammar and vocabulary you already know so you can speak more naturally.
Learn how to keep a conversation going, even if you forget a word or if there is a misunderstanding.

plan.text: Orientation: Course Introduction; Policy & Rules; Classroom expressions.
Topic 1 - Identity I : Names, majors, and the phonetic alphabet for sounds. Speaking practice in pairs and small groups.
Topic 2 - Identity II: Hometowns and types of accommodation. Speaking practice in pairs and small groups.
Topic 3 -Toolkit 1: Recycling Phase: Checking understanding; Spelling names. Speaking practice in pairs and small groups.
Topic 4 - Relationships I: Family members and occupations. Speaking practice in pairs and small groups.
Topic 5 - Relationships II: Friendships; characters, and temperament. Speaking practice in pairs and small groups.
Mid-term Assessment: Group interaction assessment using KU Rubric (Group A: Assessing the first half of the class).
Mid-term Assessment: Group interaction assessment using KU Rubric (Group B: Assessing the second half of the class).
Topic 6 - Food & Culture: Describing Japanese food. Speaking practice in pairs and small groups.
Topic 7 - Health & Lifestyle: Personal hygiene, exercise, and diet. Speaking practice in pairs and small groups.
Topic 8 - Free Time I: Hobbies and interests. Speaking practice in pairs and small groups.
Topic 9 - Free Time II: Sports, TV, entertainment, and media. Speaking practice in pairs and small groups.
Topic 10 - Travel I: Travel & Transport within Kochi. Speaking practice in pairs and small groups.
Topic 11 - Travel II: International Travel, Countries and nationalities. Speaking practice in pairs and small groups.
Final Assessment: Group interaction assessment using KU Rubric (Group A: Assessing the first half of the class).
Final Assessment: Group interaction assessment using KU Rubric (Group B: Assessing the second half of the class).

textbooks:
Distributed printed materials.
Eikaiwa Handbook (Essential for classroom expressions and pronunciation guides).
prereq: This course is for all first-year students. You must take this class to graduate.
prep: Students are expected to spend approximately 4 hours per week on preparation and review. This includes maintaining a vocabulary list for each topic and practicing pronunciation symbols.
teachers: ◎ ナングラ カウナパワ

extra.label: 区分1
extra.text: 平成20年度以降入学生／令和６年度以降入学生

extra.label: 区分2
extra.text: 共通教育）初年次科目／国際コミュニケーション科目基軸英語

extra.label: 履修における注意点
extra.text: １年次に必修。１外国語（英語）履修上限年間10単位。卒業にはⅠとⅡ両方が必要。

extra.label: 資格等
extra.text: 教免－省令で定める科目：外国語コミュニケーション

extra.label: この授業で身につける「10+1の能力」
extra.text:
課題探求力
語学に関するリテラシー
コミュニケーション力
協働実践力
自律力

extra.label: オフィスアワーに関する補足
extra.text: If a student want to meet with the teacher, she/he may please send the teacher an email or ask the teacher during class to set up a time.

extra.label: 教員の実務経験の有無
授業形態
extra.text:
授業中や授業時間外のグループワークを含む
その他
The student will use Moodle for some of their homework activities and task. This work helps the students to get ready for new lessons and practice what we have already studied.
Students, please check Moodle before every class to see if there is any ""Prep Work"" (homework to do before we start a new topic).
